step.title: Resolve Findings
step.goal: Walk the merged finding list in priority order (Critical first, then High, Medium, Low). For each finding, decide resolution path: self-evident → Edit directly; ambiguous → Question tool; structural → loop back. Track waivers.

substep.title: Classify the Finding
substep.action:
For {{current_finding}}, decide its resolution path:
- **Self-evident**: fix is mechanical and unambiguous. Examples: wikilink format fix; heading level fix; AC missing a verb; obvious typo in a section title.
- **Ambiguous**: fix has multiple plausible options OR carries design implications. Examples: vague AC needs sharpening (multiple ways to sharpen); risk-area citation is mismatched (which file is right?); section content overlaps another section (which to trim?).
- **Structural**: fix requires re-doing earlier-phase work. Examples: foundational contradiction needs re-grilling; missing AC because behavior was never grilled; drift from Design Summary requires re-aligning with the user.
Store as {{current_finding.resolution_path}}.

check.action:
Apply the fix via Edit tool. Update the ticket body per the finding's fix_recommendation.
Note in {{verify_log}} that the fix was applied without user consultation (self-evident class).
Continue to next finding.
Apply via Edit per fix_recommendation. Note in {{verify_log}}: "Applied per recommendation."
Apply the user's described fix via Edit. Note in {{verify_log}}: "Custom fix per user input: {{description}}."

For Medium / Low: note in {{verify_log}}: "Waived." For High: surface a confirmation — "Waiving a High finding means the ticket is degraded; the implementation agent may surface this. Confirm waiver?" — and only proceed on explicit confirmation. For Critical: refuse the waiver. "Critical findings cannot be waived; either resolve or loop back. Choose: Apply / Custom / Loop back."

Identify the loop-back target based on the finding's nature: - Foundational contradiction / un-grilled gap → ticket-grill - Drift from Design Summary / decision needs re-alignment → ticket-design - Missing required section / outline mismatch → ticket-structure - Writing-order or checkpoint issues → ticket-plan - Section content needs re-drafting → ticket-write (often possible to resolve inline as ambiguous; loop only if multiple sections need re-write)
Surface the loop-back recommendation:
"Finding {{current_finding.id}} requires looping back to {{target_skill}}. The structural fix exceeds inline editing.
Options: - Loop back (Recommended): exit ticket-verify; invoke {{target_skill}} via menu; re-run ticket-verify after the loop resolves the gap - Override: I'll attempt an inline fix even though it's structural (NOT RECOMMENDED — accept risk that the fix will be incomplete)"
On loop-back: note in {{verify_log}} which target was loop-recommended; append a section to the questions file documenting the loop-back; stamp last_updated; exit step 4 (and the skill — re-entry happens after the loop-back resolves).
On override: classify as ambiguous and re-enter substep 4c.
Note: loop-back from V-phase does NOT increment ticket-design's strike counter (the strike counter is for USER-DRIVEN foundational reversals, not verify-driven gap surfacing).
The fix introduced a frontmatter or schema issue. Halt the for-each loop. Surface the error. Manually correct or revert the offending edit before continuing.

section.title: Priority order matters
section.description: Resolve Critical findings first. They may invalidate downstream Medium/Low findings (e.g., a Critical "missing AC section" might subsume a Medium "AC heading level off" — fixing the Critical drops the Medium). Walking in priority order saves rework.

section.title: Loop-back is a clean exit, not a continuation
section.description: When a structural finding triggers loop-back, this skill EXITS. It does not stay open waiting for the loop to complete. The user invokes the target skill, that skill runs, the user re-invokes ticket-verify. Each ticket-verify invocation runs fresh self-review + dispatch + resolution — so the prior verify session's resolved findings are NOT preserved as state. The questions file's verify log is the durable record.

rules.rule: Refuse Critical waivers. Force resolution or loop-back
High waivers require explicit acknowledgment of degraded ticket
Medium / Low waivers are casual; log and continue
Multi-waiver tickets are flagged in step 5 — too many Low waivers can collectively degrade quality
Edit tool, not Write — always append/modify existing content; never overwrite the ticket file
Preserve frontmatter integrity — never edit frontmatter inadvertently while fixing body content
If a fix requires touching multiple sections, do them as separate Edit calls for clarity in version control diff
